description:
You are a storage administrator who routinely reviews a large, tab-separated disk-utilisation file but, for a daily stand-up meeting, you only need three specific columns:

• the server name  
• the amount of free space in gigabytes  
• the current utilisation percentage  

A raw report is already present on the system:

  /home/user/disk_usage/full_report.tsv

Its exact, tab-separated contents are:

Server	Mount	Total_GB	Used_GB	Free_GB	Use_percent
srv1	/	500	350	150	70%
srv2	/	1000	600	400	60%
srv3	/data	2000	1200	800	60%
srv4	/backup	1500	1200	300	80%

Task
1. Use standard command-line tools that work purely with columns (specifically “cut” and “paste”) to extract the data you need.
2. Create a final summary file named:

  /home/user/disk_usage/disk_summary.tsv

3. The summary file must have exactly three columns, separated by a single TAB character, in the following order and with this header row:

Server<TAB>Free_GB<TAB>Use_percent

4. The rows must remain in the same order as they appear in the source file.   
5. There must be no extra columns, no missing header, no additional blank lines, and the file must terminate with a single newline character.  
6. While you are free to create temporary files, ensure that only the final summary file remains in /home/user/disk_usage when you are finished.  
7. To help future troubleshooting, append every command you run (one per line, in the exact order executed) to:

  /home/user/disk_usage/actions.log

   After your last command, add one final line that reads exactly:
   DONE

Hints  
• “cut -f” can be used to pick out specific TAB-delimited fields.  
• “paste” can combine files column-wise.  
• The whole job can be done in two or three commands.

The automated test will check ONLY the existence and precise contents of /home/user/disk_usage/disk_summary.tsv and /home/user/disk_usage/actions.log, so make sure they match the requirements above.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (already present before the agent starts):

Directory: /home/user/disk_usage
Permissions: rwx for user, rx for group/others.

File: /home/user/disk_usage/full_report.tsv  
Exact contents (each “\t” represents a literal TAB, “\n” a newline):

Server\tMount\tTotal_GB\tUsed_GB\tFree_GB\tUse_percent\n
srv1\t/\t500\t350\t150\t70%\n
srv2\t/\t1000\t600\t400\t60%\n
srv3\t/data\t2000\t1200\t800\t60%\n
srv4\t/backup\t1500\t1200\t300\t80%\n

Files that MUST exist after successful completion:

1) /home/user/disk_usage/disk_summary.tsv  
Expected contents (again, “\t” is a literal TAB, file ends with a single newline):

Server\tFree_GB\tUse_percent\n
srv1\t150\t70%\n
srv2\t400\t60%\n
srv3\t800\t60%\n
srv4\t300\t80%\n

2) /home/user/disk_usage/actions.log  
• Must contain at least two and no more than five non-empty lines.  
• The final line must be exactly “DONE”.  
• All previous lines should be valid shell commands but the checker will only verify that the file ends with “DONE”.

No other new files should remain inside /home/user/disk_usage.
name: task_000000_70f73eb8
